name: feat-ESE-0015-01
title: Renderer RenderTarget and viewport APIs
description: Extend the Renderer interface and ThreeJSRenderer with APIs for render target management and viewport control. Three new capabilities: (1) create/destroy offscreen render targets by ID, (2) set the active render target (texture or browser framebuffer), (3) set viewport region for direct-to-screen rendering, (4) bind a render target texture to a mesh material.
acceptanceCriteria:
- createRenderTarget(id, width, height) creates a WebGLRenderTarget
- destroyRenderTarget(id) disposes it
- setRenderTarget(id) directs rendering to that target; setRenderTarget(null) restores browser framebuffer
- setViewport(x, y, w, h) sets viewport and scissor region
- resetViewport() restores full-window viewport
- setMaterialTexture(handle, textureId) binds a render target texture to a mesh material
- All methods added to Renderer interface and ThreeJSRenderer implementation
demoDeliverable: Terminal output from unit tests showing render target creation, viewport setting, and texture binding.
testingScenarios:
- Create a render target, verify it exists in the renderer
- Set render target, render, verify output goes to texture not screen
- Set viewport region, render, verify only that region is drawn
- Bind texture to mesh material, verify material.map is set
- Destroy render target, verify cleanup
stakeholderUnderstanding: Foundation of the view system. Setting up a mechanism to position cameras and target their outputs, for both GUI and in-world applications.
